inputs.tags:
The Sonic Landscape: Dystopian Grit
The track sits at a relentless 140 BPM. It acts as a metronome for the brain, driving forward with the mechanical precision of a military march.

The Low End: Beneath the track is a pulsing, saturated synth bass—engineered with that 40Hz Gamma wave frequency we talked about—that you feel in your chest more than you hear. It grounds the player and eliminates nervous jitters.

The Percussion: The drums don't sound like a standard kit; they sound like heavy artillery. The kick drum is a massive, compressed thud, and the snare is a sharp, gated metallic crack that mimics the sound of an empty magazine hitting a concrete floor.

The Guitars: Down-tuned, 7-string electric guitars pan hard left and right. They don’t play soaring solos; they chug in violent, staccato bursts that perfectly match the firing rate of in-game assault rifles, seamlessly blending with the game's audio engine.

The Vocals: The Drill Sergeant in Your Head
The lyrics aren't sung—they are barked with the calculated, icy aggression of a cybernetic drill instructor.

The vocalist has a deep, commanding baritone wrapped in a layer of metallic distortion and tight reverb.

During the verses ("Drop the sprint. Hold the wall."), the delivery is methodical and hypnotic, planting the instructions directly into the subconscious.

When the chorus hits ("BURST THE FIRE! RIDE THE KICK!"), the vocals explode into a full-throated, adrenaline-fueled shout, designed to spike dopamine exactly when the player breaches a room or takes an objective.

The Pacing: Controlling the Heart Rate
The song structure actively manages the player's physiological state to prevent panic:

The Verses & Pre-Chorus: The rhythm builds tension but keeps the movement grounded, forcing the player to check their corners, scan the map, and remember their trigger discipline.

The Chorus: Total sensory overload. The bass drops, the guitars form a massive wall of sound, and the track gives them the raw aggression needed to win a chaotic 1v3 firefight.

The Bridge ("Panic kills. Focus saves."): The beat drops into half-time. The heavy instrumentation pulls back, leaving just a pulsing synth and the isolated vocals. It forces the player to take a breath, reset their mental state, and plan their next rotation instead of rushing blindly back into the meat grinder.
inputs.lyrics:
[Verse 1: Overriding The Death Sprint]

Break the tunnel. Scan the line.
Cover to cover. Make it mine.
Drop the sprint. Hold the wall.
Slice the pie. Watch 'em fall.
Drop the sprint. Hold the wall.
Slice the pie. Watch 'em fall.

[Pre-Chorus: Overriding Tunnel Vision]

Eyes up. Read the map.
Check the flank. Spring the trap.
Triangle scan. Ammo. Sight.
Breathe slow. Win the fight.

[Chorus: Overriding Spray & Pray + The Reload Tic]

BURST THE FIRE! RIDE THE KICK!
CLEAR THE SECTOR! THINK QUICK!
DROP THE FIRST! HOLD THE MAG!
SWING THE CORNER! SECURE THE BAG!
BURST THE FIRE! RIDE THE KICK!
HOLD THE MAG! THINK QUICK!

[Verse 2: Overriding The Solo Hero]

Squad down. Hold the push.
Throw the smoke. Blind the bush.
Wait for backup. Hold the ground.
Crossfire set. Lock it down.
Wait for backup. Hold the ground.
Crossfire set. Lock it down.

[Bridge: The Mental Reset]

Panic kills. Focus saves.
Anchor down. Break the waves.
Calculate. Execute.
Find the angle. Plant the root.

[Outro: Muscle Memory Loop]

Slice the pie. Burst the fire.
Check the map. Push it higher.
Hold the mag. Throw the smoke.
Sector clear.
